decision.archive_summary: Earlier work on Codex minor GC harder regression coverage was completed: a natural Chinese multi-turn topic-switching case was added to fixed live evals, and the implementation was adjusted away from whitelist-like behavior by tightening the packaged guard so it cannot override shadow-guard vetoes. Durable carry-forward requirements: keep expanding harder cases like GC-B style natural Chinese multitopic turns, and avoid whitelist/hardcoded solutions.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn '继续' stays automatically and clearly continues the same active task, so relation=continue. Recent raw context around the still-active thread should remain, especially the test/retest exchange (t8-t11). Earlier implementation-confirmation turns are resolved and can leave the next-turn prompt. The durable user requirements from t4 and t6 should survive as pins because they still constrain the ongoing work even if the older raw turns are evicted.
